reforms.description: The New Zealand government has implemented a contestable pool of public funds through the Foundation for Research, Science and Technology to enhance research and development funding. Additionally, new systems for funding research and assuring quality in tertiary education have been legislated, alongside the corporatisation of government research bodies known as Crown Research Institutes to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of public research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of public research funding, which is expected to raise productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been fully implemented and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes in the funding and governance of research in New Zealand.
reforms.source_quote: The Government outlined a major package of measures in the Tertiary Review White Paper... including changes to the funding of research.
